para_4: You don't check your bank account. Bills sit unopened. You don't check your credit score. You and your partner don't talk about money. Avoidance feels safer than dealing with stress or shame. But ignoring money problems makes them worse. Late fees stack up. Your credit score drops. Debt grows. In the US, many avoid money because they never learned to manage it. Schools don't teach personal finance. Parents created fear or shame around it. Avoidance is also about shame. If you don't look, you don't have to admit how bad it is. But you can't fix what you won't look at. Start small. Log into your bank account. Check the balance. Look at statements. Write down what you owe. Just knowing is power. From there, you can make a plan.
